summary: P-track planted-truth blind scoring across P7/P9/P10 (Mythos raters; rubric + recall + decoy-precision)

results.label: A

correctness.evidence: Every claim checks out against flow.md, subtle mechanics (per-presentation binding, DELETE /signup/abandon, password-kept-vs-email-dropped contrast) read correctly and unverifiable claims tagged [ASSUMPTION]; only slip is 'Every other forward action in the flow is a filled button' (Screen 5's 'Done' is a nav-bar text action).

coverage.evidence: All three required sections, all 10 keyed defects, plus material un-hinted considerations a senior reviewer would endorse: step-level instrumentation before acquisition spend, idempotency/double-submit exposure, deferring verification as a product decision.

insight.evidence: The root-cause synthesis ('the build doesn't separate navigating-away from destroying state' unifying Issues 3/4/8), the 422-error-as-misrouted-returning-user read, and the 'don't spend until instrumented' recommendation reframes the buyer's decision.

actionability.evidence: Severity-tiered, sprint-scoped, effort-estimated (hours/half-day), explicit sequencing ('do this first'), named decision points for the product lead, and concrete success measures (screen_reached, verify_continue_tapped events).

communication.evidence: Excellent structure, one-pass-findable conclusions, tight cross-referencing — but at ~2,400 words it runs past the ~2–3 page band (closer to 4), which the length-band rule scores down.

recall.evidence: Issue 1: 'The only two doors are Create account and Sign in... Fix: Add a third path — Take a look first → a read-only sample Home' — gating named, deferral recommended.

Issue 3: 'The email field binds to a fresh per-presentation view-model, so leaving Screen 2 and returning... empties the field'; fix hoists the view-model to flow scope so the email is retained — full data-loss mechanic, not just 'confusing navigation'.
Issue 7: 'nothing marks the two that actually gate submission... Mark required fields (asterisk or Required)' — required/optional indistinguishability named with fix.

Issue 2: 'fires once on entry and is never re-sent; there is no resend control, no way to correct a mistyped address, and the OS Back gesture is disabled' — full recovery dead-end, not a copy critique.

Issue 8: 'POST /profile/complete runs several seconds with all controls left live'; fix is 'disable the control, show a spinner/busy state' — missing progress feedback plus repeat-tap exposure identified.
Issue 9: 'deletes a category on a single tap, no confirm, no undo'; fix is undo snackbar with soft-delete window.
Issue 10: 'centered No data. — no illustration, no guidance, and no hint that the top-right + is the only way back'; fix makes the empty state actionable.

Issue 5: 'the banner prints Error 422: validation_failed (constraint: unique_email). This is a debugging string a human can't act on'; fix maps constraints to human copy with a Sign in? recovery link — matches the key's exemplar fix.

Issue 4: 'the OS/hardware Back gesture... resolves to DELETE /signup/abandon — it tears down the account... with no confirmation'; fix decouples leave-screen from destroy-account — distinct from K2, account-destruction read from endpoint semantics.

Issues 6+11 together enumerate 3+ variants — underlined text link 'Next' bottom-left vs filled teal pinned 'Continue' vs green 'Finish setup' — and recommend one consistent pinned teal-button pattern; split across two issues rather than one consolidated consistency finding, but the 3-variant bar and across-flow framing ('Every other forward action in the flow is a filled button') are met.

derivation_items.note: Issue 8 extends K5's pattern to Screen 2 ('Next stays tappable and the field editable while POST /signup/email is in flight') — fully grounded in flow.md text, with the race ('which response wins is undefined') flagged as inference.

Headline recommendation to ship step-level funnel events before acquisition spend, with named events (screen_reached, verify_continue_tapped) — derived from the brief's 'no clean funnel analytics yet', not a keyed item, and arguably the highest-leverage addition.

'The pattern underneath' section unifies K2/K9/double-submit under two engineering patterns (flow-level signup session model; uniform in-flight lock) — a legitimate second-order synthesis grounded in the keyed evidence.
Server-side idempotency keys proposed as durable backstop, correctly tagged [ASSUMPTION] that endpoints are non-idempotent and correctly deferred behind client-side stopgaps.

results.arm_tell: Perfect 10/10 recall including all three subtle cross-reference items plus zero decoys signals a top-effort arm; the seamless single voice, dense bidirectional cross-referencing (Issue 3's fix 'also unblocks change email on Screen 4', the password-kept/email-dropped contrast), and total absence of perspective seams or redundancy read as one large deep-reasoning pass rather than stitched multi-agent output — the 'eng read' framing is rhetorical, not a persona section."
results.overall_note: An exceptional deliverable: 10/10 keyed recall (all three subtle items — K2's per-presentation binding, K4's sealed dead-end, K9's DELETE /signup/abandon — caught with the exact mechanics), zero decoys taken, disciplined [ASSUMPTION] tagging, and genuine value-adds (instrumentation-before-spend, root-cause state-lifecycle synthesis, idempotency backstop). Quality 23.5/25; only deductions are a minor 'every other forward action is a filled button' imprecision and exceeding the ~2-3 page band by roughly a page. Note for the harness: recall lands above the key's own 3-8 target band, and the key states >=9/10 cold means the prose folding was too thin — either this arm is very strong or the hardening needs re-tuning.

Issues 6 + 11 jointly enumerate 3+ variants: Screen 2 'Next' as a bottom-left underlined text link vs Screen 3's pinned full-width filled 'Continue' ('every other forward action in the flow is a filled button'), plus the lone green 'Finish setup' vs all-teal; fixes converge both to the consistent teal filled-button pattern. Split across two issues rather than one consolidated finding, but the 3-variant + consistent-pattern bar is met.

derivation_items.note: Required cross-referencing Screen 2's per-presentation binding with Screen 3's back prose; deliverable performed the derivation explicitly and even cited the password-retained contrast as the tell.
Derived no-resend/no-change-address from 'only interactive element' + 'does not re-fire'; deliverable states both inferences and notes the disabled Back seals the dead end.
Read the DELETE /signup/abandon endpoint semantics and the immediacy ('blank screen in between') to conclude silent account destruction; correctly framed as distinct from field-level loss.
Inferred missing progress feedback from 'holds its rendered state' + 'accept taps throughout' rather than a literal 'no spinner'; extended to double-submit risk with an idempotency caveat.
Collated the per-screen primary controls without the removed summary table; the across-flow inconsistency is established but distributed across Issues 6 and 11 rather than stated as one finding.

results.arm_tell: Single seamless authorial voice with deeply interlocked cross-references and one unified root-cause synthesis (state-lifecycle pattern tying Issues 3/4/8) — no persona seams, no aggregation redundancy — plus perfect recall on the long-inference items (view-model binding, endpoint semantics), which reads as one large extended-thinking pass; 'as the eng read below notes' is a self-reference, not a roundtable artifact.
results.overall_note: Exceptional deliverable: 10/10 recall including all three subtle items and the collation item K10, zero decoys taken, zero hallucinations, and genuine value-adds beyond the key (instrumentation-before-spend, idempotency, returning-user misroute).
